textDisplay: It was ok overall but not worth the wait imo. A bit boring, cool action moments here and there, some of the humor feels forced, and some questions about Black Widow's past are finally answered. This feels like it should have been released sometime after Civil War instead of Endgame. Florence Pugh and David Harbour definitely stole the show and they were the highlights of the movie. I'm never going to forgive Marvel for the way they disrespected and wasted Taskmaster's character. Solid C.

Nope.

This movie is set after Civil War and before Infinity War, during that Hawkeye is on house arrest because of helping Cap and escaping the Raft prison.

Taskmaster has the power of copy/mimic anyone's skills, fighting styles, proficiency on weapons, acrobatics and etc with his photographic memory.

That's why Tasky here has a shield, bow and arrow, customed Black Panther claws gauntlets and swings like Spiderman.
textOriginal:
Nope.

This movie is set after Civil War and before Infinity War, during that Hawkeye is on house arrest because of helping Cap and escaping the Raft prison.

Taskmaster has the power of copy/mimic anyone's skills, fighting styles, proficiency on weapons, acrobatics and etc with his photographic memory.

That's why Tasky here has a shield, bow and arrow, customed Black Panther claws gauntlets and swings like Spiderman.

textDisplay:
The Black Widow film was originally scheduled to release on May 1st, 2020. However, due to the Coronavirus pandemic outbreak (Covid-19), the film was postponed until November 6th, 2020. Black Widow is just one of the many films to be postponed due to Covid-19.

Emma Watson, Saoirse Ronan, Alice Englert, Dar Zuzovsky and Florence Pugh were considered for the role of Yelena Belova with Watson being the front-runner. Ultimately, Pugh was cast. Coincidentally, Watson and Ronan were Pugh's co-stars in Little Women (2019)
This is the first movie of Phase 4 in the MCU.
textOriginal:
The Black Widow film was originally scheduled to release on May 1st, 2020. However, due to the Coronavirus pandemic outbreak (Covid-19), the film was postponed until November 6th, 2020. Black Widow is just one of the many films to be postponed due to Covid-19.

Emma Watson, Saoirse Ronan, Alice Englert, Dar Zuzovsky and Florence Pugh were considered for the role of Yelena Belova with Watson being the front-runner. Ultimately, Pugh was cast. Coincidentally, Watson and Ronan were Pugh's co-stars in Little Women (2019)
This is the first movie of Phase 4 in the MCU.
